summary:
Claim: Lymphoma is the most common mediastinal tumor.  
Evidence: - Non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma (particularly diffuse large B-cell) accounts for 50%–60% of mediastinal malignancies, especially in young adults. - Thymomas, though common in the midline mediastinum, make up less than half of all mediastinal tumors.  
Answer: C
